message:
docs: Add Feature 44 Functional Module Coherence comprehensive specification

Add exhaustive specification for Feature 44: Functional Module Coherence
& Stoichiometry Checker (~1800 lines of detailed documentation):

Concept overview:
- Segment genomes into functional modules (replication, morphogenesis,
  lysis, lysogeny, host interaction, auxiliary)
- Evaluate stoichiometric balance (capsid:scaffold, tail fiber sets)
- Flag incomplete or overrepresented modules
- Suggest missing genes with homolog candidates

Mathematical foundations:
- Module Completeness Score: weighted presence/absence of essential genes
- Stoichiometric Balance Score: log-ratio deviation from expected ratios
- Module Coherence (gene neighborhood clustering)
- Expression Proxy from RBS strength and codon adaptation

Full TypeScript implementation (~400 lines):
- Type definitions: ModuleType, ModuleGene, FunctionalModule,
  StoichiometryRule, ModuleAnalysis, ModuleSuggestion
- MODULE_KEYWORDS: Regex patterns for classifying genes by function
- ESSENTIAL_GENES: Required components per module with weights
- STOICHIOMETRY_RULES: Expected gene ratios with biological rationale
- analyzeModuleCoherence(): Main analysis orchestration
- classifyGeneToModule(): Keyword-based module assignment
- computeCompletenessScore(): Weighted presence scoring
- computeStoichiometryScore(): Ratio deviation analysis
- computeModuleCoherence(): Gene neighborhood clustering
- generateSuggestions(): Gap identification and prioritization

TUI visualization mockup:
- Module completeness radar chart (ASCII)
- Gene-to-module color mapping
- Stoichiometry balance bars
- Missing gene suggestions panel

Assessment ratings:
- Novelty: 8/10 (stoichiometry + TUI unique)
- Pedagogical: 9/10 (modular organization, protein ratios)
- Wow Factor: 7/10 (satisfying completeness visualization)

This feature enables "genome health checks" that identify incomplete
phages, annotation gaps, and unusual module architectures.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
